prediction.attackType: MITRE T1505.003 Server Software Component: Web Shell
prediction.summary: A critical web shell incident has been detected on the web server. An attacker from IP 10.20.8.77 successfully uploaded a PHP web shell (`shell.php`) to the `/uploads/` directory. Following the upload, the attacker executed multiple commands, including `whoami`, `cat /etc/passwd`, and attempted to download a secondary payload from `evil.tld`. The activity was flagged by WAF, IDS, and SIEM systems, with ModSecurity also blocking a subsequent SQL injection probe.

timeline.event: Attacker uploads 'shell.php' via POST request to /uploads/shell.php
WAF alerts on suspicious PHP upload to webroot /uploads/shell.php from 10.20.8.77
Attacker executes 'whoami' command via web shell
Attacker executes 'cat /etc/passwd' command via web shell
Attacker attempts to download 'stage2' payload from http://evil.tld via web shell
IDS detects web shell activity: repeated POST to .php with cmd parameter
SIEM identifies MITRE T1505.003 Server Software Component: Web Shell
ModSecurity blocks SQLi probe from 10.20.8.77 to /uploads/shell.php
SIEM issues CRITICAL alert for webshell on webserver01

recommendations: Immediately isolate the affected web server (webserver01) to prevent further compromise and lateral movement.
Perform a full forensic analysis of the web server to identify the initial compromise vector, extent of access, and any persistence mechanisms.
Remove the `/uploads/shell.php` file and any other malicious files or payloads (e.g., `stage2`) from the server.
Review web server logs (Apache, WAF, ModSecurity) for earlier activity from 10.20.8.77 and similar attack patterns.
Update WAF/IDS rules to specifically block uploads of executable files to non-executable directories and enhance command injection detection.
Block the attacker's IP address (10.20.8.77) at the perimeter firewall and investigate `http://evil.tld` for additional threat intelligence.
